items.title: Venezuela Earthquake Aftermath
items.why_it_matters: The successful rescue of Hernán Gil after eight days of entombment has provided a rare moment of relief amidst a catastrophic twin-earthquake disaster in Venezuela. While international aid remains critical to the survival of the thousands affected, survivors report that the current pace of relief distribution is insufficient. items.title: UK Forced Adoptions Apology
items.why_it_matters: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has issued a formal state apology regarding the systemic forced adoption of children from unmarried mothers between 1949 and 1976. The government has committed to providing financial support to help victims access records and facilitate family reconnection. While survivors welcome the acknowledgment, many are calling for further comprehensive mental health support to address the long-term trauma caused by these policies.
